body_txt: One character assumes another has a certain item, skill or relationship based purely on a stereotype, be it racial, gender, or orientation-based. For instance, someone asks a gay character if he can design clothes, or a Latino character if he has any relatives who work on cars, or a black character if he knows where to buy weed. The recipient is invariably offended that they would believe such bigoted assumptions, and will usually rebuke them vocally and at length. A common extension to this gag is for the recipient, after having delivered their vigorous rebuttal, to reveal that they do in fact have the desired skill or item readily at hand . This allows writers to indulge ethnic and gender assumptions with built-in Lampshade Hanging . Compare I Resemble That Remark! . Contrast with Stop Being Stereotypical , Mistaken for Racist , Discriminate and Switch . Also see Hypocritical Humor .
